Scenario.overview: Communications Complex A sophisticated threat actor group C0015 targets the communications industry using a multi-stage attack plan involving AI-powered reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Target Artifact (Tasks) OS Process File User Account Credential Store Internet Scan Alternate
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Artificial Intelligence Response Metadata (Mechanism) Process Injection Scheduled Task/Job Bootkit File Metadata (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Response Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses artificial intelligence to gather information about the target's tasks.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker acquires infrastructure through internet scans.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-0798 (Suggested): Microsoft Office contains a memory corruption vulnerability due to the way objects are handled in memory. Successful exploitation allows for remote code execution in the context of the current user. This vulnerability is known to be chained with CVE-2018-0802.
stage.description: The attacker gains initial access through spear phishing via services.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-43770 (Suggested): Roundcube Webmail contains a persistent cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability that can lead to information disclosure via malicious link references in plain/text messages.
stage.description: The attacker gains initial access through drive-by compromise.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2025-57819 (Suggested): Sangoma FreePBX contains an authentication bypass vulnerability due to insufficiently sanitized user-supplied data allows unauthenticated access to FreePBX Administrator leading to arbitrary database manipulation and remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker executes malicious code through process injection.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-46748 (Suggested): F5 BIG-IP Configuration utility contains an SQL injection vulnerability that may allow an authenticated attacker with network access through the BIG-IP management port and/or self IP addresses to execute system commands. This vulnerability can be used in conjunction with CVE-2023-46747.
stage.description: The attacker executes malicious code through scheduled tasks/jobs.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker establishes persistence through bootkits.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker evades detection through command obfuscation.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker accesses credentials through brute force attacks.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker discovers sensitive information through network sniffing.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally through valid accounts.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects data through staged data.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker achieves impact through software footprinting.

Scenario.summary: This complex attack scenario involves multiple stages, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact. The attacker uses various techniques, including artificial intelligence, spear phishing, drive-by compromise, process injection, scheduled tasks/jobs, bootkits, command obfuscation, brute force attacks, network sniffing, valid accounts, data staging, and exfiltration over alternative protocols.
